description: Test vectors for batch and sync checksums. A checksum is the lowercase SHA-256 hex digest of the canonical form of the payload. Canonicalisation is defined in 03-replication.md and is distinct from JSON.stringify. All implementations must produce identical checksums for identical input.

canonicalisation.float: a finite non-integer number is its JSON number; a non-finite number is null
canonicalisation.object: keys sorted ascending, keys with an absent value skipped, each rendered as "key":value

batch_checksum.name: Single INSERT change
batch_checksum.note: The checksum is computed over the canonical form of the whole changes array, not per change. Object keys are sorted and integers are tagged with __sint.

sync_batch_checksum.name: Two rows of user data
sync_batch_checksum.note: Sync batch, schema, and manifest checksums use the same canonicalisation over their payload.

notes: Canonicalisation sorts object keys ascending, so field insertion order does not affect the digest.
An integer value is tagged as {"__sint":"<decimal>"}; a finite non-integer number serialises as a plain JSON number, for example 42.5.
A byte array is tagged as {"__blob":"<base64>"}.
An object key whose value is absent is skipped; a null value is kept as null.
The pure-language SHA-256 used by the reference implementation and a standard crypto SHA-256 produce identical digests for these inputs.
